goal: Set visibility='invisible' on all 10 helper Xform prims under /World/Helpers/ in a single batch_set_attributes call (not 10 individual set_attribute calls). Verify via get_attribute samples.
thoughts: Bulk ops are cheaper over Kit RPC — batch_set_attributes is the single-call primitive for this. USD visibility is an attribute with token values 'invisible' / 'inherited', NOT a bool. Confirm the count first, then set, then sample a few to verify.
code:
# 1. Confirm count
count_prims_under_path(parent_path="/World/Helpers", type_filter="Xform")

# 2. Single bulk call (note: 'invisible' is a token, not a bool)
paths = [f"/World/Helpers/Helper_{i}" for i in range(10)]
batch_set_attributes(
    prim_paths=paths,
    attr_name="visibility",
    value="invisible"
)

# 3. Sample-verify
get_attribute(prim_path="/World/Helpers/Helper_0", attr_name="visibility")
get_attribute(prim_path="/World/Helpers/Helper_9", attr_name="visibility")
failure_modes: Looping set_attribute 10 times — works but is 10x slower over RPC and violates the 'use the batch tool' principle.
Setting visibility=false or 0 — USD visibility is a token, not a bool; the write silently does nothing or errors.
Deleting the prims instead of hiding them — visibility=invisible preserves the prims for re-enabling later.
Forgetting to verify — batch_set_attributes may partially fail on some paths; sample-verify catches it.
